pattern: Incrementing every element but one is equivalent to decrementing that one, so the minimum moves equal the total distance from each element down to the array minimum.
hints: Raising all-but-one by one is the same, relatively, as lowering a single element by one. Reframe the operation that way.
If you only ever lower elements, the cheapest target is the current minimum, since you never want to go below it.
Sum over all elements of (value - min); that count of single-element decrements equals the required number of all-but-one increments.

edge_inputs.description: negative values with a low minimum
one large outlier dominates the move count
wide spread of negative and positive extremes

stress.description: large arrays with values that keep the total within a 32-bit answer
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    base = rng.randint(-1000, 1000)
    return [[base + rng.randint(0, 20000) for _ in range(n)]]
stress.note: narrow spread keeps the summed distance inside the guaranteed 32-bit answer
